channel.title: CyberNetSec - Threat Actor
channel.description: Latest threat actor threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: Data-Wiping Attacks on the Rise, Abusing Legitimate Admin Tools
item.description:
The supply chain industry is being warned about a rising trend of destructive data-wiping attacks. A key example is the March 2026 incident at medical tech firm Stryker, where the Iranian hacktivist group Handala Hack Team claimed to have used Microsoft Intune to issue remote wipe commands to 80,000...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Cyberattack, Threat Actor, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Alkegen Data Breach Exposes Social Security Numbers, Health Records
item.description:
Specialty materials manufacturer Alkegen (formerly ASP Unifrax Holdings) has disclosed a data breach that may have exposed the Social Security numbers and personal health records of affected individuals. The incident, first claimed by the Akira ransomware group on April 23, 2026, allegedly involved ...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Ransomware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: Exposed Server Reveals Attacker's AI-Powered Phishing Toolkit
item.description:
Researchers at Rapid7 discovered an unsecured server that exposed a sophisticated, AI-assisted toolkit for phishing and malware delivery. The operator, believed to be Russian-speaking, was targeting Windows users in Mexico with an infostealer. The toolkit shows extensive use of Large Language Models...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Phishing, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: China-Aligned APTs Use Public AI Tools in Espionage Campaigns
item.description:
State-sponsored threat actors aligned with China are integrating publicly available AI tools, such as Anthropic's Claude Code and DeepSeek, into their cyber-espionage campaigns. A report from Hunt.io details how the APT groups used these tools as core components to automate attacks, generate malicio...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Threat Intelligence, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: 'SleeperGem' Supply Chain Attack Hits RubyGems via Dormant Accounts
item.description:
A sophisticated supply chain attack named 'SleeperGem' has targeted the RubyGems ecosystem by hijacking dormant maintainer accounts. Attackers published malicious versions of at least three gems, including the popular 'fastlane-plugin-run_tests_firebase_testlab', to distribute a persistent backdoor....

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: New 'LegacyHive' Windows zero-day LPE exploit published online
item.description:
A security researcher has publicly disclosed 'LegacyHive,' an unpatched zero-day vulnerability in the Windows User Profile Service (ProfSvc). The flaw allows for local privilege escalation (LPE) on fully updated Windows 11 and Windows Server systems. The researcher, 'Nightmare Eclipse,' released pro...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Actor, Malware

Read Full Article →

item.title: New Email Attacks Use Real Microsoft Login Pages to Bypass MFA
item.description:
Security researchers are warning about increasingly sophisticated email attacks, including the Tycoon 2FA Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platform. This adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) attack uses a reverse proxy to present the victim with a real Microsoft login page. When the user authenticates and co...

Severity: High

Categories: Phishing, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: Ransomware Roundup: LockBit, Akira, and Others Claim Victims Across Multiple Sectors
item.description:
The BreachSense threat intelligence platform has documented a significant wave of data breaches on July 1, 2026, attributed to a diverse set of active ransomware groups. The victims span multiple industries and geographies, including companies in technology, government, and manufacturing. Prominent ...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Data Breach, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: EU & UK Sanction Russian GRU, FSB Officers in Joint Cyber Action
item.description:
The European Union and the United Kingdom have implemented their first joint sanctions package against Russian cyber operations, targeting 24 individuals and entities. The sanctions name senior officers from Russia's GRU and FSB intelligence services, formally attributing attacks on Poland's energy ...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Policy and Compliance, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Akira Ransomware Claims Attack on Marketing Firm Ironmark
item.description:
The Akira ransomware group has claimed responsibility for a cyberattack on Ironmark, a U.S.-based marketing and communications provider. On July 13, 2026, the group added Ironmark to its dark web leak site, threatening to release 190GB of stolen data. The compromised information allegedly includes s...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Data Breach, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →
